name: Pumpkin Brownines/Ice Cream/Caramel Sauce
recipeInstructions:
These untraditional brownies don't contain chocolate but they sure taste great. Dense, moist, and cakelike, they have a pumpkin flavor perfect for Halloween. Makes 10.
Preheat oven to 350°F. Butter and flour an 8-inch square glass baking dish. Using an electric mixer, beat butter in a large bowl until light. Gradually add brown sugar and beat until well blended, about 2 minutes. Add 1 egg and 1 teaspoon vanilla and beat to blend. Add flour, baking powder, spices, and salt and beat until well mixed. Beat in pumpkin. Stir in nuts. Spread batter in prepared pan (batter will be thick).
Mix cream cheese, 2 tablespoons sugar, egg yolk, whipping cream, and remaining 1/2 teaspoon vanilla in a bowl to blend. Drop cream cheese mixture by heaping tablespoons atop batter. Using a small knife, gently swirl the cream cheese mixture into the batter, creating a marbled pattern.
Bake until a tester inserted into the center comes out clean and the top is firm, about 35 minutes. (Can be made ahead and kept covered at room temperature.) Cool. Cover with foil and let stand at room temperature. Reheat covered in a 350°F oven for 15 minutes before serving. Cut warm brownies into squares. Arrange on plates. Top with a scoop of ice cream. Drizzle with caramel sauce.
